---
title: "Report on App Development Companies"
author: "Sujashree Roy"
date: "2026-05-25"
output: html_document
---

VISUALIZATION FOR APP DEVELOPMENT

```{r}
# =========================
# 1. Load Libraries
# =========================
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

# =========================
# 2. Load Excel File
# =========================
data <- read_excel("~/Downloads/APP DEV .xlsx")

#Set the first row as column names now
colnames(data) <- data[1, ]

# Check column names
colnames(data)
```

```{r}
# =========================
# 3. Clean & Split Industries
# =========================
data_clean <- data %>%
  separate_rows(Industries, sep = ",") %>%   # split multiple industries
  mutate(Industries = str_trim(Industries))  # remove spaces

# =========================
# 4. Create Industry Categories
# =========================
data_clean <- data_clean %>%
  mutate(Industry_Category = case_when(
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Retail|Fashion|eCommerce", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "eCommerce",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("SaaS|Software|IT|Tech", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "IT/Software",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Healthcare|Medical|Pharma", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Healthcare",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Finance|Banking|FinTech", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Finance",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Real Estate|Property", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Real Estate",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Education|EdTech", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Education",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Marketing|Advertising|Media", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Marketing",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Travel|Tourism|Hospitality", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Travel & Hospitality",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Logistics|Supply Chain", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Logistics",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Manufacturing|Industrial", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Manufacturing",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Sports", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Sports",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Automotive", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Automotive",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Food|Restaurant|Beverage|F&B", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Food & Beverage",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Gaming|Game", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Gaming",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Energy|Oil|Gas", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Energy",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Insurance", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Insurance",
    
    str_detect(Industries, regex("Government", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Government",
    
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# =========================
# 5. REMOVE DUPLICATES (VERY IMPORTANT)
# =========================
data_unique <- data_clean %>%
  distinct(data_clean$`Company Name`, Industry_Category)

# =========================
# 6. Count Industries (Correct Way)
# =========================
industry_counts <- data_unique %>%
  count(Industry_Category, sort = TRUE)
industry_counts_filtered <- industry_counts %>%
  filter(Industry_Category != "Other")
print(industry_counts_filtered)
```

```{r}
# =========================
# 7. Plot Industry Distribution
# =========================
ggplot(industry_counts_filtered, aes(x = reorder(Industry_Category, n), y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Industry Distribution (Unique Companies)",
       x = "Industry",
       y = "Number of Companies")

# =========================
# 8. (Optional) Check what's inside "Other"
# =========================
data_clean %>%
  filter(Industry_Category == "Other") %>%
  count(Industries, sort = TRUE)
```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)

clients_clean <- data %>%
  separate_rows(Clients, sep = ",") %>%   # split multiple clients
  mutate(Clients = str_trim(Clients))     # remove spaces
```

```{r}
unique_clients <- clients_clean %>%
  distinct(Clients)
print(unique_clients)
nrow(unique_clients)
unique_clients <- clients_clean %>%
  filter(!str_detect(Clients, regex("startup|sme|small|medium|enterprise|business", ignore_case = TRUE))) %>%
  distinct(Clients)
top_clients <- clients_clean %>%
  count(Clients, sort = TRUE)

print(top_clients)
```

```{r}
top_clients <- clients_clean %>%
  count(Clients, sort = TRUE) %>%
  filter(n > 1) %>%   # remove single mentions
  slice_head(n = 10)

num_clients <- nrow(unique_clients)
client_freq <- clients_clean %>%
  count(Clients)
print(paste("Total Unique Clients:", num_clients))

ggplot(client_freq, aes(x = n)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "#F58518") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Client Frequency Distribution",
       x = "Number of Mentions",
       y = "Number of Clients")

library(ggplot2)

top_clients <- clients_clean %>%
  count(Clients, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

top_clients <- top_clients %>%
  filter(!str_detect(Clients, regex("startup|sme|small|medium|enterprise|business", ignore_case = TRUE)))
ggplot(top_clients, aes(x = reorder(Clients, n), y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#F58518") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Top 10 Clients (Excluding Generic Terms)",
       x = "Client",
       y = "Number of Mentions")
```


```{r}
# Count unique companies per industry
company_count <- data_unique %>%
  count(Industry_Category)

# Count total mentions (demand)
demand_count <- data_clean %>%
  count(Industry_Category)

# Merge both
industry_analysis <- merge(company_count, demand_count, by = "Industry_Category")

colnames(industry_analysis) <- c("Industry", "Companies", "Demand")

# Remove 'Other'
industry_analysis_clean <- industry_analysis %>%
  filter(Industry != "Other")

# Plot
library(ggrepel)

ggplot(industry_analysis_clean, aes(x = Companies, y = Demand, label = Industry)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "#4C78A8") +
  geom_text_repel() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Industry Opportunity Matrix",
       x = "Competition",
       y = "Demand")
```
